title: Graveyard Transfer
summary: A late-night FTP session moved more than it should have, and the useful evidence is buried in the data stream.
scenario: A contractor claimed they only uploaded harmless logs during a night shift. The packet capture shows an FTP control session plus a short-lived high-numbered data connection that transferred a suspicious file. Students must reconstruct the transferred object from the capture, inspect the recovered payload, and identify the embedded flag.
learningObjectives: Map control traffic to a separate FTP data channel
Reconstruct transferred evidence from packet captures

artifacts.name: graveyard_transfer.pcap
artifacts.purpose: Primary network capture containing control and data streams

artifacts.name: incident_memo.txt
artifacts.purpose: Narrative note describing the suspicious transfer window

hints: The interesting bytes are probably not on port 21.
Recover the transferred file before trying to search for the flag.

llmHandoff.scope: This folder should contain the full PCAP-based challenge bundle and solution notes.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Generate a realistic FTP capture where the flag is only recoverable after reconstructing the transferred artifact from the data channel.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not require malware reversing beyond simple strings or metadata unless difficulty is intentionally raised.
